text.title: Trade-off Stakeholder Communication Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when briefings drift from the source decision. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Exec-summary reads as a clean win; PM-brief mentions "minor caveat"; engineer-note has the real risk paragraph. Production incident later catches the exec by surprise.

root-cause.source: Internal — post-mortem audit of 14 architecture decisions

antipattern.root-cause: Author softened wording for the exec audience; convergence-review gate was skipped.
antipattern.fix: Require the identical risk paragraph (verbatim string match) in all four artefacts; fail validation otherwise. See rule risk-preservation.

antipattern.symptom: Briefing addresses "stakeholders" generically; same body reused across exec/PM/engineer/ops with cosmetic header swap. Nobody acts on it because nobody owns it.
antipattern.root-cause: Stakeholder roles never identified by name; agent invented generic personas.
antipattern.fix: Block bundle generation when stakeholder map is missing role → name → primary concern. See rule role-fit-required.

antipattern.symptom: One subagent emits all four artefacts in a single pass; nobody verifies convergence. Risk wording quietly diverges between exec and engineer.

root-cause.source: Internal — close-rate audit on ADR briefings 2025

antipattern.root-cause: Author of the technical analysis is not a reliable editor of their own summary.
antipattern.fix: Route convergence-check sub-task to a separate model (opus) with explicit prompt "do the four artefacts agree on the same risk?"; fail if no. See rule convergence-review-gate.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer-note lists what we chose but not what we gave up; six months later a new engineer "fixes" by adding the rejected option back, undoing the trade.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats sacrifice as obvious; future readers do not see the rejected branch.
antipattern.fix: Require sacrificed[] array with ≥1 entry in engineer_note; validator rejects empty arrays. See rule no-hidden-sacrifice.
